feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to Implement Digital Signatures in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Invoices & POs)?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We have a requirement to implement digital signatures for business documents such as Tax Invoices and Purchase Orders (print outputs) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

While exploring possible solutions, I came across SAP Signature Management by DocuSign and reviewed the following resources:

  • https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2602/SolP/1XV?region=IN

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ff01156df84f470cb46483b8a0ba0b79/2d27519421144e92bc09cb223ab18c1e.html?locale=en-US

I have a few questions:

  1. Is SAP Signature Management by DocuSign supported for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud output documents (like invoices and POs)?

  2. What is the end-to-end process/architecture to implement this (especially for print forms)?

  3. Does it require SAP BTP integration or middleware for this scenario?

  4. Are there any standard SAP-supported approaches for embedding digital signatures directly in output forms (Adobe Forms)?

  5. If DocuSign is not feasible, what are the alternative approaches recommended in Public Cloud?

Any guidance, implementation steps, or real project experience would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support!

Thanks,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud 2602 /Authorization Concept for the App List of Supplier Invoices
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
i need help with the following scenario:
The goal is, that a user can access the app List of supplier invoices and that he can view all invoices and attachements, but cant create, edit or cancel anything. The second aspect is, that the user should still be able to recieve workflow tasks for invoices that are Saved as Complete and is able to accept or deny them. 

The issue for in this case is, if i change the custom business role, that i created, and restrict the role to only read access and value help access, the user is no longer able to recieve and work with workflow tasks (as this counts as an edit to the supplier invoice).

My second idea was to create an app variant and remove unwanted buttons. This almost worked but the user is still able to cancel posted invoices, if he uses the app list of supplier invoices. If the user navigates to the invoice from the workflow task, then he enters F7900 and can even post or edit supplier invoices. Based on my tests, im not even able to make UI Adaptions to F7900 and prohibit the user from using these functions.

My question is, how can i proceed from here. Are there any other tools/settings that allow me to restrict the user on this granular level ? Can some restrictiontypes be used for this and not only for different access levels for example by Purchase Organization ? 

Im thankfull for any suggestions.

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: "Tax account could not be determined for YCOA/&/&/&/&" - (FOT_TXA020)
entry.content:
When releasing a billing document to accounting in App Change Billing Document (VF02) the system raises error “Tax Account could not be determined for YCOA/&/&/&/&” Message no. FOT_TXA020

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Best Practice for Sales Service with Inbound Material Flow in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm looking for the "Fit-to-Standard" approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for a specific business case:

  1. Service Sales: We provide a waste collection service.

  2. Reverse Logistics: Instead of shipping goods, we receive material (waste) from the customer into our plant.

  3. Invoicing: We need to invoice the customer for the collection service (Debit Memo).

In the legacy ECC, they used a "Sales Order -> Inbound Delivery" workaround. Since we want to keep the Clean Core, what is the recommended Scope Item or flow?

  • Should I use Advanced Customer Returns (23B) and trigger a Debit Memo instead of a Credit Memo?

  • Is there a way to combine Lean Services (J14) with an automated inbound movement?

  • Or is there a specific "Reverse Logistics for Service" scenario in Public Cloud?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Billing Plan Restrictions in Quantity Contracts
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently working on implementing a rental contract scenario in SAP Public Cloud.

In SAP On-Premise, we used rental contracts (e.g. periodic billing scenarios via contracts with billing plans). However, in the Public Cloud, the available contract types are mainly limited to Quantity Contracts and Value Contracts.

1. Mapping of Rental Contract Scenario

Given this limitation, I would like to better understand the recommended approach:

  • What is the recommended standard object in SAP Public Cloud to represent a rental / periodic billing contract?
  • Should this scenario be modeled using Quantity Contracts with Billing Plans, or is there a more suitable alternative (e.g. subscription-based solutions (subscription billing) or other scope items)?

2. Billing Plan on Header vs. Item Level

In the Public Cloud system, I noticed that the Billing Plan can only be maintained at item level.
In SAP On-Premise, it was possible to maintain Billing Plans at header level as well.

  • Is there any configuration or workaround in the Public Cloud to enable Billing Plans at header level for Quantity Contracts?

3. Item Category Change Requirement

When creating a Billing Plan, it appears necessary to change the item category in order to enable billing plan functionality.

  • Is this the standard and expected behavior in SAP Public Cloud?
  • Are there recommended best practices to avoid manual item category adjustments?

4. Billing Plan Type Restrictions

The system defaults the Billing Plan Type to S5, and I am unable to modify it.

  • In SAP On-Premise, it was possible to define and assign different Billing Plan Types.
  • Is the Billing Plan Type fixed in the Public Cloud?
  • If so, is there any SSCUI or configuration path that allows influencing or extending the available Billing Plan Types?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Kathi

entry.title: Need a workflow to review and approve the sales return order.
entry.content:
The requirement is that the client needs a workflow to review and approve sales return orders in the SAP Public Cloud System.

Detailed Step-by-Step:

  1. The client requires a workflow to be implemented in the system.
  2. This workflow should handle sales return orders.
  3. The workflow must include a review step, where the relevant personnel can examine the sales return order.
  4. After the review, there should be an approval step to authorize the sales return order.
  5. The entire process should be configured and executed within the SAP Public Cloud system.

entry.title: Restrict VPN access at the user level so that the user can log in to the system .
entry.content: How to restrict VPN access at the user level so that the user can log in to the system only through the assigned VPN in SAP S4hana Public cloud.

entry.title: Unable to find CDS view for MSME
entry.content:
Which CDS view for MSME number that can be used to maintain in the business partner company code supplier: country specific tab in app maintain business partner.

If standard CDS view not available please suggest CDS view to create custom CDS view.

entry.title: How to enable an automatic email of an Invoice, IRN and E-waybill PDF's
entry.content:
How to enable an automatic emailing of invoice and IRN/E-waybill PDFs to multiple recipients (e.g., Dispatch In-charge, ASM and Stores).

Step-by-Step Requirement Description

  1. Document Generation
    • The system should generate the following documents upon billing:
      • Customer Invoice (PDF)
      • IRN (Invoice Reference Number) document
      • E-Way Bill document (PDF)
  2. Automatic Email Trigger
    • Once the billing document is successfully created, an automatic email should be triggered by the system without manual intervention.
  3. Multiple Recipients Handling
    • The email should be sent to multiple recipients, including:
      • Dispatch In-charge
      • Area Sales Manager (ASM)
      • Stores Department

entry.title: Mandatory Billing from Primary Storage Locations Across All Plants
entry.content:
How to Enforce Mandatory Billing from Primary Storage Locations Across All Plants

Step 1:
The client requires billing to be done only from the main storage location in each plant.

Step 2:
The organization operates 27 plants, and this rule applies to all of them.

Step 3:
Billing must not be done from any other storage location.

Step 4:
The client requires that all billing activities be carried out exclusively from the designated main storage location within each plant.

Final Step 5:
The client requires that all billing activities be carried out exclusively from the designated main storage location within each plant. The organization operates 27 plants, and for each plant, billing must strictly originate from its respective primary storage location. Under no circumstances should billing be processed from any alternate storage location.

entry.title: Possibility to change status of purchasing documents
entry.content:
As far as I know it is not possible to change the statuses of the purchasing documents (ex. purchase requisition). In the current flow of the customer the purchase requisition starts in the application "my purchase requisitions" (F1639A). When a requestor creates a purchase requisition it goes in to the status "approved", which is not in line with the flow. After the requestor has created this purchase requisition, another person with more authorizations should enhance the purchase requisition. 

Still the status of the requisition of the first person is "approved", while it should be something like "submitted". Is there any possibility to change the status names in the system accordingly?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: S4 Public Cloud x MBC Setup
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm currently trying to connect an S4 Public Cloud system to MBC. Right now, I'm in the process of configuring the MBC settings directly in S4. The configuration guide and documentation keep referring to an MBC connector. Can you tell me where this connector is located? Directly in S4 or in the MBC tenant? It’s a bit confusing because I thought I had to make the settings in S4, but there are constant references to an MBC connector whose settings are supposed to override the configuration in S4 and make it optional.

Thanks for your help

entry.title: Intermittent Non-Delivery of Emails from “Schedule Billing Output” (F1510)
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

When we attempt to send billing documents directly to customers via a job created in the “Schedule Billing Output” app (F1510), we have noticed that even when the document shows the status “Sent” in the system, customers sometimes do not receive the emails. Is there anything that could be blocking these messages?

Additionally, does the system validate whether the email addresses are valid?

Thank you very much.

entry.title: Creating Production Order Component Line Items via API
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

We are currently working on a Custom Application Developed using the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) model in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, where Batch wise line items are being managed.

As part of our requirement, we are using the A_ProductionOrderComponent_4 API to update values in the CO02 Production Order screen. However, this API does not fully meet our needs.

At present, when using the PATCH method, we are only able to modify existing line items and are unable to create new ones.

Our requirement is to create new line items in the Production Order Component along with the corresponding batch details. We would like to understand if there is any POST method or appropriate API available to support this functionality. Alternatively, we would appreciate any recommendations for a suitable approach to achieve this requirement.

Thank you,

Production order Component line Item

entry.title: Project Budget not flexible to add budget-based schedules in Exisiting Project
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

Project Budget not flexible to add budget-based schedules in Exisiting Project

In S/4HANA Public Cloud release, there is any supported way to “add new WBS elements to an existing enterprise project using the standard Excel templates (Import Financial Plan Data / Project Budget, etc.) 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud release, there is any supported way to “add new WBS elements to an existing enterprise project using the standard Excel templates (Import Financial Plan Data / Project Budget, etc.) 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: API for : Manage Budget Documents - Project app
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Our client would like to automate updation of Project Budgets data via an API.

We can see a Fiori app "Manage Budget Documents - Project " which can be used but client would like to mimic its functionality via an API(interface). Do we have an API in S4 Public cloud for this requirement?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: requirement dates in capacity scheduling table
entry.content:
We are working with EDI scheduling agreements in SAP  S/4HANA  Public Cloud and noticed the following behavior:

In the Capacity Scheduling Table app, the requirement date always shows a time of 00:00, even though a different time is maintained in the scheduling agreement release (delivery schedule).

Is there any way to influence or adjust this time, or is it always fixed to 00:00 because planning in S/4HANA Public Cloud is performed only on a daily basis (date‑level planning)?

entry.title: Transport Calendar
entry.content: I'm not able to transport Factory Calendar for further systems. How can I do it?

entry.title: Delivery partner details from CDS not released for development
entry.content:
The CDS views to fetch the delivery partner details such as I_DeliveryDocumentPartner, I_DeliveryPartnerAddress, I_OutboundDeliveryPartnerTP are not released to use for development.

Please provide any accessible CDS views for the same.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
